clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Exclude white-coat hypertension using out-of-office BP monitoring before diagnosing hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with untreated office SBP 130-159 mmHg or DBP 80-99 mmHg (excluding SBP ≥160 or DBP ≥100)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Out-of-office BP monitoring recommendation with COR 2a + LOE B-NR

navigationPath.purpose: Record office SBP and DBP values
Show COR 2a recommendation for out-of-office monitoring
Provide instructions for ambulatory or home BP monitoring
Schedule repeat assessment after out-of-office data collection
Compare office vs out-of-office readings for final diagnosis

validationRules: Patient must be untreated (no antihypertensive meds)

rules: Check if office SBP between 130-159 mmHg OR DBP between 80-99 mmHg
Verify office SBP is NOT ≥160 mmHg AND DBP is NOT ≥100 mmHg
Confirm patient is not on antihypertensive medication
If criteria met → recommend out-of-office BP monitoring (COR 2a, LOE B-NR)
Out-of-office options: ambulatory BP monitoring (ABPM) or home BP monitoring (HBPM)
Compare office readings with out-of-office averages
If office elevated but out-of-office normal → diagnose white-coat hypertension
If both elevated → diagnose sustained hypertension

recommendation.description: It is reasonable to exclude white-coat hypertension using out-of-office BP monitoring before diagnosis

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Order ambulatory BP monitoring or provide home BP monitoring instructions
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Repeat assessment after 7-14 days of out-of-office monitoring
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Compare office reading with out-of-office average (typically 2-3 readings per day for 7 days)
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if office BP ≥160/100 for immediate diagnosis without out-of-office monitoring
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log COR 2a recommendation, LOE B-NR, and monitoring plan to health record
